source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Cloud Storage Bucket Is Not Anonymously or Publicly Accessible

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud Storage Bucket Is Not Anonymously or Publicly Accessible
input_row.description: It is recommended that IAM policy on Cloud Storage bucket does not allows anonymous or public access.
input_row.rationale: Allowing anonymous or public access grants permissions to anyone to access bucket content. Such access might not be desired if you are storing any sensitive data. Hence, ensure that anonymous or public access to a bucket is not allowed. Impact: No storage buckets would be publicly accessible. You would have to explicitly administer bucket access.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Storage browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. Click on each bucket name to go to its Bucket details page. 3. Click on the Permissions tab. 4. Ensure that allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers are not in the Members list. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all buckets in a project gsutil ls 2. Check the IAM Policy for each bucket: gsutil iam get gs://BUCKET_NAME No role should contain allUsers and/or allAuthenticatedUsers as a member. Using Rest API 1. List all buckets in a project Get https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b?project=<ProjectName> 2. Check the IAM Policy for each bucket GET https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b/<bucketName>/iam No role should contain allUsers and/or allAuthenticatedUsers as a member.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Storage browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. Click on the bucket name to go to its Bucket details page. 3. Click on the Permissions tab. 4. Click Delete button in front of allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers to remove that particular role assignment. From Google Cloud CLI Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers access. gsutil iam ch -d allUsers gs://BUCKET_NAME gsutil iam ch -d allAuthenticatedUsers gs://BUCKET_NAME Prevention: You can prevent Storage buckets from becoming publicly accessible by setting up the Domain restricted sharing organization policy at: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/orgpolicies/iam- allowedPolicyMemberDomains . Default Value: By Default, Storage buckets are not publicly shared. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam-reference 2. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/making-data-public 3. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/gsutil/commands/iam Additional Information: To implement Access restrictions on buckets, configuring Bucket IAM is preferred way than configuring Bucket ACL. On GCP console, "Edit Permissions" for bucket exposes IAM configurations only. Bucket ACLs are configured automatically as per need in order to implement/support User enforced Bucket IAM policy. In-case administrator changes bucket ACL using command-line(gsutils)/API bucket IAM also gets updated automatically.
input_row.impact: No storage buckets would be publicly accessible. You would have to explicitly administer bucket access.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam-reference 2. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/making-data-public 3. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/gsutil/commands/iam Additional Information: To implement Access restrictions on buckets, configuring Bucket IAM is preferred way than configuring Bucket ACL. On GCP console, "Edit Permissions" for bucket exposes IAM configurations only. Bucket ACLs are configured automatically as per need in order to implement/support User enforced Bucket IAM policy. In-case administrator changes bucket ACL using command-line(gsutils)/API bucket IAM also gets updated automatically.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

Based on the provided fields and audit instructions, this control is best approached through automation. The process involves checking IAM policies associated with Google Cloud Storage buckets to ensure that neither `allUsers` nor `allAuthenticatedUsers` are members. This type of check can be efficiently performed using GCP APIs or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, enabling an automated solution.

**DECISION CRITERIA:**
- GCP APIs are available to query IAM policies for Cloud Storage buckets.
- The control relates to access control settings, a standard GCP service property.
- Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK and CLI have commands to retrieve IAM policies.
- The audit instructions explicitly mention using Google Cloud CLI (`gsutil iam get`) and REST API endpoints for checking IAM configurations.
- This control does not require SSH/RDP access or involve application-level or OS-level checks.

Given these considerations, the control is suitable for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_access

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

The automated program will:

1. Enumerate all storage buckets within a GCP project.
   - Use the Google Cloud Storage REST API to list buckets: `GET https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b?project=<ProjectName>`
   - Alternatively, use the `gsutil ls` command to list bucket names.

2. For each bucket, retrieve the IAM policy:
   - Use the REST API: `GET https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b/<bucketName>/iam`
   - Alternatively, employ the `gsutil iam get gs://<BUCKET_NAME>` command.

3. Examine the IAM policy for the presence of members `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`.
   - Ensure no roles include these members, which would indicate public access.

4. Pass/Fail Criteria:
   - **Pass**: The IAM policy does not list `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` in any roles.
   - **Fail**: The IAM policy includes `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`, granting public or anonymous access.

By executing these steps, the program ensures that no Cloud Storage buckets are publicly accessible, aligning with the compliance control requirements.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can and should be checked via automation.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capability:** The GCP Storage API, `gsutil`, and `gcloud` commands all support retrieving bucket IAM policies. The control does not require manual review or context not available via API.
- **Other GCP Tools:** While CloudFormation is AWS-specific, GCP's equivalent (Deployment Manager) is not relevant for this runtime check. GCP Config (now "Config Controller") does not natively monitor public access on buckets, so direct API/CLI checks are best.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:** The steps described are technically accurate and follow best practices.
    - Listing buckets: `gsutil ls` or Storage API is correct.
    - Retrieving IAM policies: `gsutil iam get gs://<BUCKET_NAME>` and REST API are both valid.
    - Checking for `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` is the correct logic.
- **gcloud Usage:** While `gsutil` is the standard tool for Storage, `gcloud storage` is now GA and could also be referenced for completeness.
- **Program Name:** `gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_access` is descriptive, but could be more specific (see below).
- **Completeness:** The review does not mention **Bucket Policy Only** (Uniform bucket-level access) or **Public Access Prevention** settings, which are additional GCP features that can enforce no-public-access at the bucket level. These should be checked for completeness and defense-in-depth.
- **Edge Cases:** The assessment does not mention ACLs, which are deprecated but could still exist on older buckets. The focus on IAM is correct for new buckets, but a note about legacy ACLs would be prudent.

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Include Public Access Prevention:** Recommend checking the `publicAccessPrevention` setting (`enforced` is most secure) via API or `gsutil bucketpolicyonly get`.
- **Consider Uniform Bucket-Level Access:** Check if `iamConfiguration.uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled` is set to `true` for defense-in-depth.
- **Legacy ACLs:** Note that while IAM is the primary mechanism, legacy ACLs could still grant public access. Consider including a check for `READER` or `OWNER` permissions to `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` via `gsutil acl get`.
- **gcloud storage:** Reference `gcloud storage buckets describe <BUCKET_NAME>` as an alternative to `gsutil` for IAM and public access prevention settings.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** For clarity and alignment with naming conventions, consider:
    - `gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_iam_access`
    - `gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_access_iam`
    - `gcp_storage_bucket_public_access_prevention_enforced`
    - If including all checks: `gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_access_all_methods`
- **Explicitly Document Pass/Fail Logic:** Specify that *any* occurrence of `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` in *any* role is a fail, regardless of role type.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP security best practices.
- Minor improvements would make it more robust and future-proof.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** **Modify**

**Suggested Modifications:**
- Add checks for `publicAccessPrevention` and `uniformBucketLevelAccess`.
- Optionally, check for legacy ACLs if supporting older buckets.
- Update program name for clarity, e.g., `gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_iam_access`.
- Reference `gcloud storage` as an alternative to `gsutil`.

**Summary:**  
The assessment is largely correct and suitable for automation, but should be expanded to include additional GCP mechanisms that prevent public access (public access prevention, uniform bucket-level access, legacy ACLs). These additions will ensure comprehensive coverage of the control.

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that this control can be fully automated using GCP APIs and CLI tools. The IAM policy bindings for Cloud Storage buckets are accessible via the Google Cloud Storage API, `gsutil`, and `gcloud storage` commands. The audit requires only checking for the presence of `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` in bucket IAM policies—information that is directly and reliably exposed via these APIs. No manual inspection or context outside of API-accessible data is required.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_storage_bucket_no_public_iam_members

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Cloud Storage Buckets
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - List buckets: `gsutil ls` or `gcloud storage buckets list`
  - Get IAM policy: `gsutil iam get gs://<BUCKET_NAME>` or `gcloud storage buckets get-iam-policy <BUCKET_NAME>`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - In the IAM policy bindings, check all `members` fields for the presence of `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`.
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - No IAM policy binding for any bucket contains `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` as a member.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Any IAM policy binding for any bucket contains `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` as a member.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the AI opinions. The second opinion suggested a slightly more descriptive program name and mentioned additional GCP features (like Public Access Prevention), but the core compliance check per the CIS control is strictly about IAM policy members. The final program name reflects this specificity.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
